01_initial_request.target_name: Cotton secretome (Gossypium hirsutum cultivar Zhongzhimian No.2)
01_initial_request.input_data: Genome sequence of Verticillium wilt-resistant Gossypium hirsutum cultivar Zhongzhimian No.2 (ZZM2)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To determine the role of the cotton secretome in disease resistance against Verticillium dahliae
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identification of secreted proteins (2085 predicted) and validation of resistance contribution via susceptibility assays after gene silencing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The ZZM2 genome sequence (2.33 Gb, 95,327 predicted coding sequences) is available. [Gap] Secreted proteins need to be identified from the genome. [Decision] To achieve secretome identification, chose to use SignalP 5.0 to perform signal peptide prediction on predicted coding sequences, expecting to obtain proteins with signal peptides.

parameters.input: 95,327 predicted coding sequences from ZZM2 genome

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 8383 proteins identified with signal peptide sequence

references.citation: Almagro Armenteros JJ et al., 2019, Nat Biotechnol
references.relevance: SignalP 5.0 improves signal peptide predictions using deep neural networks

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 8383 proteins with signal peptides identified. [Gap] Subcellular localization needs to be predicted to confirm extracellular location. [Decision] To achieve localization prediction, chose to use WoLF PSORT to perform subcellular localization analysis on signal peptide-containing proteins, expecting to obtain extracellular localization predictions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 3879 proteins had characteristics of extracellular localization

references.citation: Horton P et al., 2007, Nucleic Acids Res
references.relevance: WoLF PSORT is a protein localization predictor

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 3879 proteins with extracellular localization predicted. [Gap] Transmembrane domains need to be excluded to confirm secreted proteins. [Decision] To achieve transmembrane domain identification, chose to use TMHMM 2.0 to perform transmembrane domain prediction, expecting to obtain proteins without transmembrane motifs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 74,991 proteins identified without transmembrane motifs

references.relevance: TMHMM evaluates methods for prediction of membrane spanning regions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] TMHMM 2.0 identified 74,991 proteins without transmembrane motifs. [Gap] Additional transmembrane domain prediction needed for validation. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive transmembrane domain identification, chose to use Phobius to perform transmembrane domain prediction, expecting to obtain complementary results.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 76,025 proteins identified without transmembrane motifs

references.citation: Käll L et al., 2007, Nucleic Acids Res
references.relevance: Phobius provides combined transmembrane topology and signal peptide prediction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Signal peptide, localization, and transmembrane domain predictions completed. [Gap] Final secretome needs to be compiled by combining all criteria. [Decision] To achieve final secretome identification, chose to use manual integration to combine SignalP, WoLF PSORT, TMHMM, and Phobius results, expecting to obtain final secreted protein list.

parameters.criteria: signal peptide + no transmembrane domain + extracellular location
parameters.input: Results from SignalP 5.0, WoLF PSORT, TMHMM 2.0, Phobius

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 2085 genes (2.19%) predicted to encode secreted proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 2085 secreted proteins identified. [Gap] Ortholog relationships need to be analyzed for conservation study. [Decision] To achieve ortholog clustering, chose to use OrthoMCL to perform ortholog group clustering, expecting to obtain orthologous groups among encoded proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ortholog groups clustered using two strategies

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] OrthoMCL clustering strategy defined. [Gap] Pairwise sequence similarities need to be calculated for clustering. [Decision] To achieve sequence similarity calculation, chose to use BLASTP to perform all-by-all pairwise sequence comparisons, expecting to obtain similarity scores for ortholog clustering.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Pairwise sequence similarities calculated for all input protein sequences

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BLASTP results obtained for ortholog clustering. [Gap] Synteny relationships need to be visualized. [Decision] To achieve synteny visualization, chose to use Circos to perform synteny analysis drawing, expecting to obtain visual representation of ortholog synteny among chromosomes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Synteny of orthologs among chromosomes drawn

references.citation: Krzywinski M et al., 2009, Genome Res

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 2085 secreted proteins identified and ortholog relationships analyzed. [Gap] Functional characteristics need to be predicted. [Decision] To achieve functional annotation, chose to use InterProScan to perform conserved domain prediction, expecting to obtain domain/motif annotations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 1495 genes have 443 conserved domains/motifs (IPR accessions)

references.relevance: InterProScan 5 for genome-scale protein function classification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Conserved domains predicted for secretome. [Gap] Gene Ontology enrichment needs to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve GO enrichment analysis, chose to use GO-seq package to perform Gene Ontology analysis, expecting to obtain functional enrichment results.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Secretome enriched in carbohydrate binding, peroxidase activity, and response to stress

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GO analysis completed. [Gap] Pathway enrichment needs to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve pathway analysis, chose to use KOBAS to perform KEGG pathway analysis, expecting to obtain pathway enrichment results.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 613 genes matched to 63 pathway accessions, enriched in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and carbohydrate metabolism

references.citation: Kanehisa M, Goto S, 2000, Nucleic Acids Res

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional annotation completed. [Gap] Transcriptome response to V. dahliae needs to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve transcriptome profiling, chose to use Illumina HiSeq 2000 to perform RNA-Seq sequencing, expecting to obtain gene expression data.

parameters.samples: 12 samples (ZZM2 and Junmian No.1 at 6, 12, 24, 48, 72h post-inoculation)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: RNA-Seq data generated for resistant and susceptible cultivars

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RNA-Seq data generated. [Gap] Reads need to be mapped to reference genome. [Decision] To achieve read mapping, chose to use Tophat2 to perform transcriptome alignment, expecting to obtain mapped reads.

parameters.reference genome: G. hirsutum cv. Zhongzhimian No.2 (GenBank: JAMQUR010000000)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Clean reads mapped onto reference genome

references.citation: Kim D et al., 2013, Genome Biol
references.relevance: Tophat2 for accurate alignment of transcriptomes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Tophat2 mapping completed. [Gap] Read alignment needs to be performed. [Decision] To achieve read alignment, chose to use Bowtie 2 to perform gapped-read alignment, expecting to obtain aligned reads.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Reads aligned to reference genome

references.citation: Langmead B, Salzberg SL, 2012, Nat Methods

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Reads aligned to reference genome. [Gap] Gene expression values need to be calculated. [Decision] To achieve expression quantification, chose to use Cuffdiff to perform FPKM calculation, expecting to obtain gene expression values.

parameters.metric: FPKM (Fragments per kilobase of transcript, per million mapped reads)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: FPKM values calculated for genes in each sample

references.citation: Trapnell C et al., 2010, Nat Biotechnol
references.relevance: Cuffdiff for transcript assembly and quantification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FPKM values calculated. [Gap] Differentially expressed genes need to be identified. [Decision] To achieve DEG identification, chose to use manual filtering to identify differentially expressed secretome members, expecting to obtain DEGs meeting significance criteria.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 627 members of predicted secretome were differentially expressed

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 627 DEGs identified in secretome. [Gap] Immune response activation needs to be tested. [Decision] To achieve immune response testing, chose to use PVX vector pGR107 to perform transient expression assays, expecting to obtain recombinant plasmids for agro-infiltration.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recombinant plasmids constructed for transient expression

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Recombinant plasmids constructed. [Gap] Proteins need to be expressed in plant tissue. [Decision] To achieve protein expression, chose to use Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101 to perform agro-infiltration, expecting to obtain transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

parameters.buffer: 10 mM MgCl2, 10 mM MES, 200 mM acetosyringone, pH 5.6

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 213 secretome members agro-infiltrated into N. benthamiana leaves

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Agro-infiltration completed. [Gap] Defense gene expression needs to be measured. [Decision] To achieve defense gene quantification, chose to use RT-qPCR to perform gene expression analysis, expecting to obtain relative expression levels of defense markers.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 28 randomly selected members induced defense response marker genes to varying degrees

references.relevance: 2^-ΔΔCt method for relative gene expression analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Immune response activation confirmed. [Gap] Resistance contribution needs to be validated via gene silencing. [Decision] To achieve gene silencing, chose to use pTRV2 vector to perform virus-induced gene silencing, expecting to obtain silenced plants for 13 selected secretome members.

parameters.plant: 2-week-old G. hirsutum cv. Zhongzhimian No.2 seedlings

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 13 secretome member genes significantly decreased in expression

references.citation: Gao XQ et al., 2011, J Vis Exp

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Gene silencing achieved. [Gap] Silenced plants need to be challenged with pathogen. [Decision] To achieve pathogen challenge, chose to use V. dahliae Vd991 to perform inoculation, expecting to obtain disease symptoms for resistance evaluation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Silenced plants inoculated with V. dahliae

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Plants inoculated with V. dahliae. [Gap] Disease symptoms need to be evaluated. [Decision] To achieve disease evaluation, chose to use disease index scoring to perform symptom assessment, expecting to obtain resistance/susceptibility classification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 11 of 13 secretome gene silenced plants exhibited Verticillium wilt symptoms; GhSec011 and GhSec039 showed higher resistance

references.citation: Fan R et al., 2017, Fungal Genet Biol
references.relevance: Established disease index for Verticillium wilt evaluation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Disease symptoms evaluated. [Gap] Cell wall lignification needs to be examined. [Decision] To achieve lignin detection, chose to use Wiesner reagent to perform histochemical analysis, expecting to obtain lignin accumulation visualization.

parameters.incubation: 10 min in phloroglucinol, 5 min in HCl

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Higher lignification in xylem vessels and interfascicular fibers in resistant cultivar ZZM2 than susceptible cultivar Junmian No.1

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Gene silencing assays (VIGS) combined with pathogen inoculation and disease index evaluation

secretome_identification.interpretation: Successfully identified 2085 secreted proteins from ZZM2 genome (2.19% of 95,327 predicted coding sequences)

differentially_expressed_genes.interpretation: 627 secretome members were differentially expressed in response to V. dahliae infection

resistance_validation.interpretation: 11 of 13 selected secretome members conferred Verticillium wilt resistance when silenced (increased susceptibility), confirming their role in disease resistance

immune_activation.interpretation: 28 randomly selected secretome members activated defense response marker genes (NbHIN1, NbPR1, NbLOX4) to varying degrees

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. The study successfully identified 2085 predicted secreted proteins from the ZZM2 genome and validated their resistance contribution through VIGS assays showing that 11 of 13 tested secretome members conferred Verticillium wilt resistance in cotton. Transcriptome analysis and immune response assays further confirmed the secretome's role in disease resistance through extracellular stress response activation and cell wall lignification.
